apis.name: Seq_First
apis.type: {T : Type} → (xs : List T) → xs.length > 0 → T
apis.nl_description: Return the first element of a non-empty sequence.

apis.name: Seq_DropFirst
apis.type: {T : Type} → (xs : List T) → xs.length > 0 → List T
apis.nl_description: Drop the first element of a non-empty sequence.

apis.name: Seq_Last
apis.type: {T : Type} → (xs : List T) → xs.length > 0 → T
apis.nl_description: Return the last element of a non-empty sequence.

apis.name: Seq_DropLast
apis.type: {T : Type} → (xs : List T) → xs.length > 0 → List T
apis.nl_description: Drop the last element of a non-empty sequence.

apis.name: Seq_ToArray
apis.type: {T : Type} → List T → Array T
apis.nl_description: Convert a sequence to an array with the same elements.

apis.name: Seq_IndexOf
apis.type: {T : Type} → [DecidableEq T] → (xs : List T) → (v : T) → v ∈ xs → Nat
apis.nl_description: Return the first index of an element known to occur in a sequence.

apis.name: Seq_IndexOfOption
apis.type: {T : Type} → [DecidableEq T] → List T → T → Option Nat
apis.nl_description: Return the first index of an element, or none when absent.

apis.name: Seq_LastIndexOf
apis.type: {T : Type} → [DecidableEq T] → (xs : List T) → (v : T) → v ∈ xs → Nat
apis.nl_description: Return the last index of an element known to occur in a sequence.

apis.name: Seq_LastIndexOfOption
apis.type: {T : Type} → [DecidableEq T] → List T → T → Option Nat
apis.nl_description: Return the last index of an element, or none when absent.

apis.name: Seq_Remove
apis.type: {T : Type} → (xs : List T) → (pos : Nat) → pos < xs.length → List T
apis.nl_description: Remove the element at a valid position.

apis.name: Seq_RemoveValue
apis.type: {T : Type} → [DecidableEq T] → List T → T → List T
apis.nl_description: Remove the first occurrence of a value, if present.

apis.name: Seq_Insert
apis.type: {T : Type} → (xs : List T) → T → (pos : Nat) → pos ≤ xs.length → List T
apis.nl_description: Insert a value at a valid sequence position.

apis.name: Seq_Reverse
apis.type: {T : Type} → List T → List T
apis.nl_description: Return the sequence in reverse order.

apis.name: Seq_Repeat
apis.type: {T : Type} → T → Nat → List T
apis.nl_description: Return a constant sequence of a given length.

apis.name: Seq_Unzip
apis.type: {A B : Type} → List (A × B) → List A × List B
apis.nl_description: Split a sequence of pairs into the pair of component sequences.

apis.name: Seq_Zip
apis.type: {A B : Type} → (xs : List A) → (ys : List B) → xs.length = ys.length → List (A × B)
apis.nl_description: Zip two equal-length sequences into a sequence of pairs.

apis.name: Seq_Max
apis.type: (xs : List Int) → xs.length > 0 → Int
apis.nl_description: Return the maximum integer in a non-empty sequence.

apis.name: Seq_Min
apis.type: (xs : List Int) → xs.length > 0 → Int
apis.nl_description: Return the minimum integer in a non-empty sequence.

apis.name: Seq_Flatten
apis.type: {T : Type} → List (List T) → List T
apis.nl_description: Flatten a sequence of sequences from left to right.

apis.name: Seq_FlattenReverse
apis.type: {T : Type} → List (List T) → List T
apis.nl_description: Flatten a sequence of sequences from right to left.

apis.name: Seq_Map
apis.type: {T R : Type} → (T → R) → List T → List R
apis.nl_description: Map a total function over a sequence.

apis.name: Seq_MapWithResult
apis.type: {T R E : Type} → (T → Result R E) → List T → Result (List R) E
apis.nl_description: Map a failure-compatible function over a sequence, stopping at the first failure.

apis.name: Seq_Filter
apis.type: {T : Type} → (T → Bool) → List T → List T
apis.nl_description: Keep the sequence elements satisfying a predicate.

apis.name: Seq_FoldLeft
apis.type: {A T : Type} → (A → T → A) → A → List T → A
apis.nl_description: Fold a sequence from the left.

apis.name: Seq_FoldRight
apis.type: {A T : Type} → (T → A → A) → List T → A → A
apis.nl_description: Fold a sequence from the right.

apis.name: Seq_FlatMap
apis.type: {T R : Type} → (T → List R) → List T → List R
apis.nl_description: Optimized implementation of flattening after mapping.

specs.nl_description: First returns the element at index 0 of a non-empty sequence.
specs.lean_form: ∀ {T : Type} (xs : List T) (h : xs.length > 0), impl.sequences.Seq_First xs h = xs.get ⟨0, h⟩
specs.name: spec_Seq_First___ensures_spec
spec_Seq_LemmaLast
specs.nl_description: Dropping the last element and appending it reconstructs a non-empty sequence.
Last returns the element at index length - 1 of a non-empty sequence.
specs.lean_form: ∀ {T : Type} (xs : List T) (h : xs.length > 0), impl.sequences.Seq_Last xs h = xs.get ⟨xs.length - 1, Nat.sub_lt h (by decide)⟩
specs.name: spec_Seq_Last___ensures_spec
spec_Seq_DropFirst___ensures_spec
specs.nl_description: DropFirst returns the suffix after the first element and reconstructs the original sequence with First.
specs.lean_form: ∀ {T : Type} (xs : List T) (h : xs.length > 0), impl.sequences.Seq_DropFirst xs h = xs.drop 1 ∧ [impl.sequences.Seq_First xs h] ++ impl.sequences.Seq_DropFirst xs h = xs

specs.name: spec_Seq_LemmaAppendLast
specs.nl_description: The last element of xs ++ ys is the last element of non-empty ys.

specs.name: spec_Seq_LemmaConcatIsAssociative
specs.nl_description: Sequence concatenation is associative.

specs.name: spec_Seq_LemmaSplitAt
specs.nl_description: Splitting a sequence at a valid position and concatenating the slices gives the original sequence.

specs.name: spec_Seq_LemmaElementFromSlice
specs.nl_description: Elements of a valid slice agree with the corresponding original elements.

specs.name: spec_Seq_LemmaSliceOfSlice
specs.nl_description: A slice of a slice equals the corresponding direct slice of the original sequence.

specs.name: spec_Seq_LemmaCardinalityOfSet
specs.nl_description: The set of elements of a sequence has cardinality at most the sequence length.

specs.name: spec_Seq_LemmaCardinalityOfEmptySetIs0
specs.nl_description: Converting a sequence to a set is empty exactly when the sequence is empty.

specs.name: spec_Seq_LemmaNoDuplicatesInConcat
specs.nl_description: Concatenating two duplicate-free disjoint sequences preserves no-duplicates.

specs.name: spec_Seq_LemmaCardinalityOfSetNoDuplicates
specs.nl_description: A duplicate-free sequence has the same cardinality as its set of elements.

specs.name: spec_Seq_LemmaNoDuplicatesCardinalityOfSet
specs.nl_description: If a sequence and its set of elements have the same cardinality, the sequence has no duplicates.

specs.name: spec_Seq_LemmaMultisetHasNoDuplicates
specs.nl_description: Each element of a duplicate-free sequence occurs exactly once.

specs.name: spec_Seq_ToArray___ensures_spec
specs.nl_description: ToArray preserves sequence length and every element position.
specs.lean_form: ∀ {T : Type} (xs : List T), let arr := impl.sequences.Seq_ToArray xs; arr.size = xs.length ∧ ∀ i, i < xs.length → arr[i]? = xs[i]?
semantic_bridge_required: Dafny arrays are represented by Lean Array values; freshness is runtime allocation and is not a scored observable in Lean.

specs.name: spec_Seq_IndexOf___ensures_spec
specs.nl_description: IndexOf returns the first valid index containing the requested value.

specs.name: spec_Seq_IndexOfOption___ensures_spec
specs.nl_description: IndexOfOption returns the first index when present and none exactly when absent.

specs.name: spec_Seq_LastIndexOf___ensures_spec
specs.nl_description: LastIndexOf returns the last valid index containing the requested value.

specs.name: spec_Seq_LastIndexOfOption___ensures_spec
specs.nl_description: LastIndexOfOption returns the last index when present and none exactly when absent.

specs.name: spec_Seq_Remove___ensures_spec
specs.nl_description: Removing at a valid position shortens the sequence and preserves surrounding elements.

specs.name: spec_Seq_RemoveValue___ensures_spec
specs.nl_description: RemoveValue returns the sequence obtained by removing the first occurrence of the value, or the original sequence when absent.
specs.lean_form: ∀ {T : Type} [DecidableEq T] (xs : List T) (v : T), let ys := impl.sequences.Seq_RemoveValue xs v; ys = xs.erase v ∧ (v ∉ xs → ys = xs) ∧ (v ∈ xs → Seq_Count v ys + 1 = Seq_Count v xs) ∧ (∀ x : T, x ≠ v → Seq_Count x ys = Seq_Count x xs) ∧ (Seq_HasNoDuplicates xs → Seq_HasNoDuplicates ys ∧ Seq_ToSet ys = (Seq_ToSet xs).filter (fun x => x ≠ v))

specs.name: spec_Seq_Insert___ensures_spec
specs.nl_description: Insert adds an element at the requested position and preserves the multiset.

specs.name: spec_Seq_Reverse___ensures_spec
specs.nl_description: Reverse has the same length and mirrors element positions.

specs.name: spec_Seq_Repeat___ensures_spec
specs.nl_description: Repeat creates a constant sequence of the requested length.

specs.name: spec_Seq_Unzip___ensures_spec
specs.nl_description: Unzip produces equal-length component sequences that reconstruct each source pair.

specs.name: spec_Seq_Zip___ensures_spec
specs.nl_description: Zip creates pair elements and unzipping the result recovers the inputs.

specs.name: spec_Seq_LemmaZipOfUnzip
semantic_bridge_required: The proof argument to Zip corresponds to the Unzip length ensures clause.
specs.nl_description: Unzipping and then zipping a sequence of pairs recovers the original sequence.

specs.name: spec_Seq_Max___ensures_spec
specs.nl_description: Max returns an element of the non-empty sequence that is at least every element.

specs.name: spec_Seq_LemmaMaxOfConcat
specs.nl_description: The maximum of a concatenation is at least the maximum of each non-empty side and all elements.

specs.name: spec_Seq_Min___ensures_spec
specs.nl_description: Min returns an element of the non-empty sequence that is at most every element.

specs.name: spec_Seq_LemmaMinOfConcat
specs.nl_description: The minimum of a concatenation is at most the minimum of each non-empty side and all elements.

specs.name: spec_Seq_LemmaSubseqMax
specs.nl_description: The maximum of a non-empty slice is bounded by the maximum of the full non-empty sequence.

specs.name: spec_Seq_LemmaSubseqMin
specs.nl_description: The minimum of a non-empty slice is bounded below by the minimum of the full non-empty sequence.
Flatten returns the source-order concatenation of all component sequences.
specs.lean_form: ∀ {T : Type} (xs : List (List T)), impl.sequences.Seq_Flatten xs = xs.flatten
specs.name: spec_Seq_Flatten___ensures_spec

specs.nl_description: FlattenReverse computes the same source-order flattened sequence by recursing from the right.
specs.lean_form: ∀ {T : Type} (xs : List (List T)), impl.sequences.Seq_FlattenReverse xs = xs.flatten
specs.name: spec_Seq_FlattenReverse___ensures_spec
spec_Seq_LemmaFlattenConcat
specs.nl_description: Flatten distributes over sequence concatenation.

specs.name: spec_Seq_LemmaFlattenReverseConcat
specs.nl_description: Reverse-order flattening distributes over sequence concatenation.

specs.name: spec_Seq_LemmaFlattenAndFlattenReverseAreEquivalent
specs.nl_description: Left-to-right and right-to-left flattening produce the same flattened sequence.

specs.name: spec_Seq_LemmaFlattenLengthGeSingleElementLength
specs.nl_description: The flattened length is at least the length of any component sequence.

specs.name: spec_Seq_LemmaFlattenLengthLeMul
specs.nl_description: The flattened length is bounded by the number of sequences times a uniform component-length bound.

specs.name: spec_Seq_FlatMap___ensures_spec
specs.nl_description: FlatMap is the optimized implementation of Flatten(Map f xs) and equals List.flatMap.
specs.lean_form: ∀ {T R : Type} (f : T → List R) (xs : List T), impl.sequences.Seq_FlatMap f xs = xs.flatMap f ∧ impl.sequences.Seq_FlatMap f xs = impl.sequences.Seq_Flatten (impl.sequences.Seq_Map f xs)
semantic_bridge_required: Dafny partial function arguments are represented as total Lean functions.

specs.name: spec_Seq_Map___ensures_spec
semantic_bridge_required: Dafny partial function arguments are represented as total Lean functions.
specs.nl_description: Map preserves length and applies the function pointwise.

specs.name: spec_Seq_MapWithResult___ensures_spec
semantic_bridge_required: Dafny failure-compatible update syntax is modeled by an explicit Result inductive.
specs.nl_description: MapWithResult succeeds pointwise or returns the first failure produced while scanning the input sequence from left to right.
specs.lean_form: ∀ {T R E : Type} (f : T → Result R E) (xs : List T), match impl.sequences.Seq_MapWithResult f xs with | Result.Success result => result.length = xs.length ∧ ∀ i, i < xs.length → ∃ v, f <$> xs[i]? = some (Result.Success v) ∧ result[i]? = some v | Result.Failure e => ∃ i, i < xs.length ∧ f <$> xs[i]? = some (Result.Failure e) ∧ ∀ j, j < i → ∃ v, f <$> xs[j]? = some (Result.Success v)

specs.name: spec_Seq_LemmaMapDistributesOverConcat
semantic_bridge_required: Dafny partial function arguments are represented as total Lean functions.
specs.nl_description: Map distributes over sequence concatenation.

specs.name: spec_Seq_Filter___ensures_spec
semantic_bridge_required: Dafny partial predicate arguments are represented as total Lean predicates.
specs.nl_description: Filter returns exactly the source-order subsequence satisfying the predicate.
specs.lean_form: ∀ {T : Type} (f : T → Bool) (xs : List T), impl.sequences.Seq_Filter f xs = xs.filter f

specs.name: spec_Seq_LemmaFilterDistributesOverConcat
semantic_bridge_required: Dafny partial predicate arguments are represented as total Lean predicates.
specs.nl_description: Filter distributes over sequence concatenation.
FoldLeft is the ordinary left fold over the input sequence.
specs.lean_form: ∀ {A T : Type} (f : A → T → A) (init : A) (xs : List T), impl.sequences.Seq_FoldLeft f init xs = xs.foldl f init
specs.name: spec_Seq_FoldLeft___ensures_spec
spec_Seq_LemmaFoldLeftDistributesOverConcat
specs.nl_description: FoldLeft over concatenation equals folding the left side then the right side.

specs.name: spec_Seq_LemmaInvFoldLeft
specs.nl_description: A left-fold invariant is preserved through FoldLeft.
FoldRight is the ordinary right fold over the input sequence.
specs.lean_form: ∀ {A T : Type} (f : T → A → A) (xs : List T) (init : A), impl.sequences.Seq_FoldRight f xs init = xs.foldr f init
specs.name: spec_Seq_FoldRight___ensures_spec
spec_Seq_LemmaFoldRightDistributesOverConcat
specs.nl_description: FoldRight over concatenation folds the right side into the initial accumulator and then folds the left side.

specs.name: spec_Seq_LemmaInvFoldRight
specs.nl_description: A right-fold invariant is preserved through FoldRight.

apis.name: Seq_MergeSort_MergeSortBy
apis.type: {T : Type} → (a : List T) → (lessThanOrEq : T → T → Bool) → Relations_TotalOrdering lessThanOrEq → List T
apis.nl_description: Sort a sequence with respect to a total ordering.

apis.name: Seq_MergeSort_MergeSortedWith
apis.type: {T : Type} → (left right : List T) → (lessThanOrEq : T → T → Bool) → Relations_SortedBy left lessThanOrEq → Relations_SortedBy right lessThanOrEq → Relations_TotalOrdering lessThanOrEq → List T
apis.nl_description: Merge two sorted sequences while preserving sortedness and elements.

specs.name: spec_Seq_MergeSort_MergeSortBy___ensures_spec
specs.nl_description: MergeSortBy preserves the multiset and returns a sorted sequence.

specs.name: spec_Seq_MergeSort_MergeSortedWith___ensures_spec
specs.nl_description: MergeSortedWith preserves the multiset of both inputs and returns a sorted sequence.

apis.name: LittleEndianNat_ToNatRight
apis.type: (model : LittleEndianNat_Model) → List LittleEndianNat_uint → Nat
apis.nl_description: Interpret a little-endian sequence from least significant to most significant.

apis.name: LittleEndianNat_ToNatLeft
apis.type: (model : LittleEndianNat_Model) → List LittleEndianNat_uint → Nat
apis.nl_description: Interpret a little-endian sequence from most significant to least significant.

apis.name: LittleEndianNat_FromNat
apis.type: (model : LittleEndianNat_Model) → Nat → List LittleEndianNat_uint
apis.nl_description: Convert a natural number to its little-endian digit sequence.

apis.name: LittleEndianNat_SeqExtend
apis.type: (model : LittleEndianNat_Model) → (xs : List LittleEndianNat_uint) → (n : Nat) → xs.length ≤ n → List LittleEndianNat_uint
apis.nl_description: Extend a digit sequence with trailing zeros to a specified length.

apis.name: LittleEndianNat_SeqExtendMultiple
apis.type: (model : LittleEndianNat_Model) → (xs : List LittleEndianNat_uint) → (n : Nat) → n > 0 → List LittleEndianNat_uint
apis.nl_description: Extend a digit sequence to a length that is a multiple of n.

apis.name: LittleEndianNat_FromNatWithLen
apis.type: (model : LittleEndianNat_Model) → (n len : Nat) → PowNat (LittleEndianNat_BASE model) len > n → List LittleEndianNat_uint
apis.nl_description: Convert a natural number to a little-endian digit sequence of a specified length.

apis.name: LittleEndianNat_SeqZero
apis.type: (model : LittleEndianNat_Model) → Nat → List LittleEndianNat_uint
apis.nl_description: Generate a little-endian zero sequence of a specified length.

apis.name: LittleEndianNat_SeqAdd
apis.type: (model : LittleEndianNat_Model) → (xs ys : List LittleEndianNat_uint) → xs.length = ys.length → List LittleEndianNat_uint × Nat
apis.nl_description: Add two equal-length little-endian digit sequences and return digits plus carry.

apis.name: LittleEndianNat_SeqSub
apis.type: (model : LittleEndianNat_Model) → (xs ys : List LittleEndianNat_uint) → xs.length = ys.length → List LittleEndianNat_uint × Nat
apis.nl_description: Subtract two equal-length little-endian digit sequences and return digits plus borrow.

specs.name: spec_LittleEndianNat_LemmaToNatLeftEqToNatRight
semantic_bridge_required: Dafny subtype/value restrictions are represented by Nat values plus explicit validSeq/isUint predicates and model assumptions; concrete uint models are test-only instantiations.
specs.nl_description: The two definitions of little-endian numeric interpretation agree.

specs.name: spec_LittleEndianNat_LemmaSeqLen1
semantic_bridge_required: Dafny subtype/value restrictions are represented by Nat values plus explicit validSeq/isUint predicates and model assumptions; concrete uint models are test-only instantiations.
specs.nl_description: A one-digit sequence denotes its first digit.

specs.name: spec_LittleEndianNat_LemmaSeqLen2
semantic_bridge_required: Dafny subtype/value restrictions are represented by Nat values plus explicit validSeq/isUint predicates and model assumptions; concrete uint models are test-only instantiations.
specs.nl_description: A two-digit sequence denotes low digit plus high digit times the base.

specs.name: spec_LittleEndianNat_LemmaSeqAppendZero
semantic_bridge_required: Dafny subtype/value restrictions are represented by Nat values plus explicit validSeq/isUint predicates and model assumptions; concrete uint models are test-only instantiations.
specs.nl_description: Appending a zero most-significant digit does not change the numeric interpretation.

specs.name: spec_LittleEndianNat_LemmaSeqNatBound
semantic_bridge_required: Dafny subtype/value restrictions are represented by Nat values plus explicit validSeq/isUint predicates and model assumptions; concrete uint models are test-only instantiations.
specs.nl_description: A valid digit sequence denotes a number below base^length.

specs.name: spec_LittleEndianNat_LemmaSeqPrefix
semantic_bridge_required: Dafny subtype/value restrictions are represented by Nat values plus explicit validSeq/isUint predicates and model assumptions; concrete uint models are test-only instantiations.
specs.nl_description: The numeric value can be split into prefix and suffix contributions.

specs.name: spec_LittleEndianNat_LemmaSeqNeq
semantic_bridge_required: Dafny subtype/value restrictions are represented by Nat values plus explicit validSeq/isUint predicates and model assumptions; concrete uint models are test-only instantiations.
specs.nl_description: Distinct equal-length digit sequences have distinct numeric interpretations.

specs.name: spec_LittleEndianNat_LemmaSeqEq
semantic_bridge_required: Dafny subtype/value restrictions are represented by Nat values plus explicit validSeq/isUint predicates and model assumptions; concrete uint models are test-only instantiations.
specs.nl_description: Equal-length digit sequences with equal numeric interpretations are equal.

specs.name: spec_LittleEndianNat_LemmaSeqLswModEquivalence
semantic_bridge_required: Dafny subtype/value restrictions are represented by Nat values plus explicit validSeq/isUint predicates and model assumptions; concrete uint models are test-only instantiations.
specs.nl_description: The least-significant digit is congruent to the numeric value modulo the base.

specs.name: spec_LittleEndianNat_LemmaNatSeqNat
semantic_bridge_required: Dafny subtype/value restrictions are represented by Nat values plus explicit validSeq/isUint predicates and model assumptions; concrete uint models are test-only instantiations.
specs.nl_description: Converting a nat to a sequence and back gives the original nat.
FromNat returns the canonical valid little-endian digit sequence for n.
specs.lean_form: ∀ (model : LittleEndianNat_Model), ∀ (n : Nat), let xs := impl.sequences.LittleEndianNat_FromNat model n; LittleEndianNat_validSeq model xs ∧ impl.sequences.LittleEndianNat_ToNatRight model xs = n ∧ (n = 0 → xs = []) ∧ (n > 0 → xs = [n % LittleEndianNat_BASE model] ++ impl.sequences.LittleEndianNat_FromNat model (n / LittleEndianNat_BASE model))
semantic_bridge_required: Dafny uint subtype restrictions are represented by Nat digits plus explicit validSeq constraints.
specs.name: spec_LittleEndianNat_FromNat___ensures_spec
spec_LittleEndianNat_SeqExtend___ensures_spec
semantic_bridge_required: Dafny subtype/value restrictions are represented by Nat values plus explicit validSeq/isUint predicates and model assumptions; concrete uint models are test-only instantiations.
specs.nl_description: SeqExtend returns length n and preserves the numeric interpretation.

specs.name: spec_LittleEndianNat_SeqExtendMultiple___ensures_spec
semantic_bridge_required: Dafny subtype/value restrictions are represented by Nat values plus explicit validSeq/isUint predicates and model assumptions; concrete uint models are test-only instantiations.
specs.nl_description: SeqExtendMultiple pads with zero digits to the exact source-computed next positive multiple of n and preserves numeric interpretation.
specs.lean_form: ∀ (model : LittleEndianNat_Model), ∀ (xs : List LittleEndianNat_uint) (n : Nat) (h : n > 0), let ys := impl.sequences.LittleEndianNat_SeqExtendMultiple model xs n h; ys.length = xs.length + n - (xs.length % n) ∧ ys.length % n = 0 ∧ (∀ i, i < xs.length → ys[i]? = xs[i]?) ∧ (∀ i, xs.length ≤ i → i < ys.length → ys[i]? = some 0) ∧ impl.sequences.LittleEndianNat_ToNatRight model ys = impl.sequences.LittleEndianNat_ToNatRight model xs ∧ (LittleEndianNat_validSeq model xs → LittleEndianNat_validSeq model ys)

specs.name: spec_LittleEndianNat_FromNatWithLen___ensures_spec
semantic_bridge_required: Dafny subtype/value restrictions are represented by Nat values plus explicit validSeq/isUint predicates and model assumptions; concrete uint models are test-only instantiations.
specs.nl_description: FromNatWithLen produces exactly len valid digits representing n.

specs.name: spec_LittleEndianNat_LemmaSeqZero
semantic_bridge_required: Dafny subtype/value restrictions are represented by Nat values plus explicit validSeq/isUint predicates and model assumptions; concrete uint models are test-only instantiations.
specs.nl_description: A sequence with numeric value zero consists only of zero digits.

specs.name: spec_LittleEndianNat_SeqZero___ensures_spec
semantic_bridge_required: Dafny subtype/value restrictions are represented by Nat values plus explicit validSeq/isUint predicates and model assumptions; concrete uint models are test-only instantiations.
specs.nl_description: SeqZero returns len zero digits with numeric value zero.

specs.name: spec_LittleEndianNat_LemmaSeqNatSeq
semantic_bridge_required: Dafny subtype/value restrictions are represented by Nat values plus explicit validSeq/isUint predicates and model assumptions; concrete uint models are test-only instantiations.
specs.nl_description: Converting a valid sequence to a nat and back at the same length recovers the sequence.

specs.name: spec_LittleEndianNat_SeqAdd___ensures_spec
semantic_bridge_required: Dafny subtype/value restrictions are represented by Nat values plus explicit validSeq/isUint predicates and model assumptions; concrete uint models are test-only instantiations.
specs.nl_description: SeqAdd returns a same-length valid result and a carry bit.

specs.name: spec_LittleEndianNat_LemmaSeqAdd
semantic_bridge_required: Dafny subtype/value restrictions are represented by Nat values plus explicit validSeq/isUint predicates and model assumptions; concrete uint models are test-only instantiations.
specs.nl_description: SeqAdd matches addition of numeric interpretations plus final carry.

specs.name: spec_LittleEndianNat_SeqSub___ensures_spec
semantic_bridge_required: Dafny subtype/value restrictions are represented by Nat values plus explicit validSeq/isUint predicates and model assumptions; concrete uint models are test-only instantiations.
specs.nl_description: SeqSub returns a same-length valid result and a borrow bit.

specs.name: spec_LittleEndianNat_LemmaSeqSub
semantic_bridge_required: Dafny subtype/value restrictions are represented by Nat values plus explicit validSeq/isUint predicates and model assumptions; concrete uint models are test-only instantiations.
specs.nl_description: SeqSub matches subtraction of numeric interpretations plus final borrow.

apis.name: LittleEndianNatConversions_ToSmall
apis.type: (model : LittleEndianNatConversions_Model) → List Large_uint → List Small_uint
apis.nl_description: Convert a sequence from the large base to the small base.

apis.name: LittleEndianNatConversions_ToLarge
apis.type: (model : LittleEndianNatConversions_Model) → (xs : List Small_uint) → xs.length % (LittleEndianNatConversions_E model) = 0 → List Large_uint
apis.nl_description: Convert a sequence from the small base to the large base.

specs.name: spec_LittleEndianNatConversions_ToSmall___ensures_spec
semantic_bridge_required: Dafny subtype/value restrictions are represented by Nat values plus explicit validSeq/isUint predicates and model assumptions; concrete uint models are test-only instantiations.
specs.nl_description: ToSmall expands each large digit into E small digits.

specs.name: spec_LittleEndianNatConversions_ToLarge___ensures_spec
semantic_bridge_required: Dafny subtype/value restrictions are represented by Nat values plus explicit validSeq/isUint predicates and model assumptions; concrete uint models are test-only instantiations.
specs.nl_description: ToLarge packs every E small digits into one large digit.

specs.name: spec_LittleEndianNatConversions_LemmaToSmall
semantic_bridge_required: Dafny subtype/value restrictions are represented by Nat values plus explicit validSeq/isUint predicates and model assumptions; concrete uint models are test-only instantiations.
specs.nl_description: Converting from large to small base preserves numeric interpretation.

specs.name: spec_LittleEndianNatConversions_LemmaToLarge
semantic_bridge_required: Dafny subtype/value restrictions are represented by Nat values plus explicit validSeq/isUint predicates and model assumptions; concrete uint models are test-only instantiations.
specs.nl_description: Converting from small to large base preserves numeric interpretation.

specs.name: spec_LittleEndianNatConversions_LemmaToSmallIsInjective
semantic_bridge_required: Dafny subtype/value restrictions are represented by Nat values plus explicit validSeq/isUint predicates and model assumptions; concrete uint models are test-only instantiations.
specs.nl_description: ToSmall is injective for equal-length valid large-base sequences.

specs.name: spec_LittleEndianNatConversions_LemmaToLargeIsInjective
semantic_bridge_required: Dafny subtype/value restrictions are represented by Nat values plus explicit validSeq/isUint predicates and model assumptions; concrete uint models are test-only instantiations.
specs.nl_description: ToLarge is injective for equal-length valid small-base sequences whose lengths are multiples of E.

specs.name: spec_LittleEndianNatConversions_LemmaSmallLargeSmall
semantic_bridge_required: Dafny subtype/value restrictions are represented by Nat values plus explicit validSeq/isUint predicates and model assumptions; concrete uint models are test-only instantiations.
specs.nl_description: Converting a valid small-base sequence to large and back recovers it.

specs.name: spec_LittleEndianNatConversions_LemmaLargeSmallLarge
semantic_bridge_required: Dafny subtype/value restrictions are represented by Nat values plus explicit validSeq/isUint predicates and model assumptions; concrete uint models are test-only instantiations.
specs.nl_description: Converting a valid large-base sequence to small and back recovers it.
